title: Level 708
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 708: "THE LOST FUTURE"}}{{Cosmic Theme}}{{Notice|body=Any feedback would be greatly appreciated. Either message me or leave a comment under the page. Thanks!}}{{Alt Class|no=708|ext=3|ent=5|env=0}} '''Level 708''', known as ''The Lost Future'' is the 709th level of the Backrooms. == '''General Description''' == '''Level 708''' is a massive futuristic city filled with glowing buildings and triangle-shaped platforms that float through the sky. The city has a slightly modern aspect to it, with steel and glass being used for the majority of the buildings. There are no roads, but a large number of bridges connect different buildings. The city’s buildings are all blue, except for three red ones in the center. Wanderers report that the red buildings each house one person, theorizing these are the leaders of the city. Neither of these guesses has been confirmed. Some wanderers have also reported a purple section underground, but all research has proven this to be false. Still, the possibility must be investigated. Throughout the city, buzzing will constantly be heard, getting louder closer to the center. Cars have been replaced with teleportation and hovercraft. {{Blurimage|file=Level 708.jpg|caption=Photo of the Core of Level 708}} The city is inhabited solely by entities calling themselves Antrocks. They are surprisingly unintelligent for their level of technology. Some Antrocks, who are called elders by ordinary members of their species but prefer to be called survivors. Survivors make many claims that make them appear insane to other Antrocks, leading us to believe they are called "elders" derogatorily. However, survivors are the most intelligent members of their species, and what they tell us explains why the Antrocks are rather unintelligent compared to their technology. The city carries on for several miles until a barrier is reached. All attempts to breach it failed. The barrier forms a dome across the whole city. (Note, the barrier has been breached; additional information has been added to the bottom of the report.) Near the barrier there is a part of the city with 21st-century architecture. While the level used to be very welcoming to wanderers, it no longer is after the barrier incident. == '''Sublevels''' == === '''Metropolis''' === The red, purple, and blue parts of the city. The very center of the level. === '''Outlands''' === The part of the level outside the barrier. It stretches for thousands of miles; the inner layer is full of suburbs, and the middle layer is full of farms. The outer layer is full of anomalies and has not been explored yet. == '''Survivor’s Stories''' == (Note, this part of the report is unconfirmed but extremely likely due to it matching up with our research.) “The Survivors revealed that a long time ago, way before humanity entered the backrooms, their species had been spread throughout the backrooms. One day, they found out how to make portals between different levels, making it easy to explore everywhere. A group of them known as the Flame Collective used this technology to transport all Antrocks to this level. Following this, the Flame Collective used the portal technology to remove key parts of the Antrocks' brains, reducing them to barely sentient slaves. Like this, they’ve continued to exist for thousands of years. When asked how the survivors managed to keep their sentience, they simply answered, "By going under." Survivors also confirmed the purple parts of the city but say you need to be a survivor to enter or even see it. How the wanderers who suggested their existence found out about them is unknown.” “The Survivors have also confirmed our theories of the city’s leadership residing in the core but disagreed with our analysis of there being only one resident in each building. They claimed the Flame Collective has hundreds of members, clearly not matching up with our findings. In support of their point, they explained that while the basics lost their full sentience, they still had limited free will and that they had to be ''trained'' to follow orders, and just three people training thousands is unlikely.” “Lastly, we were told resistance does exist in the form of the Carta Accords, a group of Survivor rebels. That is all they were willing to share.” This interview was done by [[M.E.G.]] agents. The identity of the interviewee is unknown. We at the Freedom Road thank M.E.G. for their support. == '''Barrier Incident''' ==
'''Content Warning''' [[File:Warning-triangle.png|right|frameless|150px|link=]] This section contains sensitive content '''not suitable for all audiences'''. Readers including you might consider these topic(s) disturbing, anxiety inducing, or inappropriate: '''Mutilation and mass genocide.'''
{{Underline}} === '''Description of the Outlands''' === Using technology given to us by {REDACTED}, we have managed to breach the barrier. Outside of it, it looked mostly like a suburban region, though some rural areas did exist. Our first expeditions suggested a large sentient count, numbering in the hundred thousands. These creatures were known as Strasaas and were open to trade. We found out that the level extended out the barrier for several thousand miles, but only a few hundred have been explored; anything past there is full of anomalies and not safe to explore. On further analysis of their tech, we realized they had a higher tech level than the front rooms but lower than the Antrocks. After speaking to locals, we discovered a terrible civil war ravaged the region. Each side blamed the other for a loss in a war many years ago. Further investigation led us to believe that the war was against the Antrocks, and that’s the reason the Strasaas are stuck out here. They were banished. === '''Incident Begins''' === After finding out we breached the barrier to reach them, the Strasaas stopped fighting each other, realizing they had the chance for vengeance. They marched through the barrier and attacked Actrostacus. The Survivors also saw a chance and launched their revolution against the Flame. The Flame Collective deployed thousands of robots to fight both armies. Throughout the city, the three-way war caused massive casualties for all armies. Gruesome war crimes were committed by both sides, mainly between the Collective and Strasaas. Often when Strasaas's troops found a basic family, they would kill the parents and mutilate the child. In turn, any Strasaas captured by Flame Collective forces would be executed, no matter who they were. The Survivors tried to keep away from war crimes but showed little mercy for Flame Antrocks. === '''Carta Falls''' === A few weeks into the war, the Survivors launched a full-blown attack with mechanical and biological units. This army was able to seize many city blocks and resulted in many Basic Antrocks being granted their free will back with new uplift technology discovered by the Survivors. Unfortunately, the Flame Collective found out the location of Carta and launched several warheads and assaults. Carta was fully buried underground, and the Survivors either surrendered or escaped to other underground cities. === '''Strasaas Genocide''' === After the successful attack on Carta, the Flame Collective deployed thousands of new robot troops and genetically cloned Antrocks. This new army pushes the Strasaas out of the barrier and into their homelands. Following this, the Flame Collective dropped hundreds of bombs, killing thousands of civilians and injuring thousands more. Even after the Strasaas surrendered, the genocide continued. Mutilated children were hanged on their parents' doors, and parents were torn apart. Finally, the last scream echoed through the Outlands. All Strasaas had either fled the level or died. In total, the war took 700,000 lives and lasted 15 years. === Aftermath === The aftermath of the war resulted in the Antrocks having an immense hatred for outsiders. Kill on sight orders were given to all robot warriors. == Contact Reestablished == === The Meeting === After years of silence, the Freedom Road was suddenly contacted by a representative of the Carta Accords, they offered a meeting in their new capital of Penta. 2 operatives were sent. When they returned, they told road leadership of a grand city nearly as large as Acrostacus. They estimated nearly 50,000 survivors live there. The Carta Accords’ leaders were surprisingly cordial, a direct contrast to their former silence. They explained that the reasoning for their silence was the barrier incident, but upon watching the Freedom Road’s fight thought maybe not all outsiders were bad. After hours of discussion, road operatives were informed that the Carta accords population has greatly increased, and were preparing to reclaim the surface. However, they needed help from the road’s backers (M.E.G.). The operatives agreed to seeing what they can do and left. == '''Entities''' == === '''Antrocks''' === Basic—Antrocks who lost their sentience due to the Flame Collective. Can be found everywhere except the core of the city. They are passive creatures. While they are incapable of defending themselves, all robots nearby will immediately attack any aggressor. Survivor—Indistinguishable from Basic Antrocks, these Antrocks survived the Flame Collective’s attack by “going under.” While they can be encountered on the surface, their main population is below. Flame—Instead of the blue skin of Basics and Survivors, Flame Antrocks are bright orange. They are extremely hostile and will attack anyone they see. They control Basic Antrocks and have free will. They solely live in the core of the city. === Strasaas === A formerly great species that lived in the outlands. They were great warriors, but had an immense hatred for the Antrocks. This mindless hatred caused their fall. All remaining Strasaas either reside with the Freedom Road or fled the level. == '''Colonies and Outposts''' == === '''Acrostacus''' === * Owned by the Flame Collective * Around 200,000 Antrocks reside here. * Divided into a blue and red section * Neutral but open to trade * Hostile and closed to trade following the barrier incident === '''Cartra''' === * ''Owned by the Carta Accords'' * ''Population unconfirmed'' * ''In the purple part of the level'' * ''Friendly and open to trade'' * Destroyed after barrier incident === Freedom Post === * Owned by the Freedom Road * Population 6000 * In the suburban part of the level * Friendly and open to trade === Penta === * Owned by the Carta Accords * Population 50,000 * In purple part of the level * Friendly but only open to trade with members of the Freedom Road == Civilizations == === Flame Collective === An oligarchic republic based in Acrostacus. It has a constitution, separation of powers, and an elected president. However, only Flame Antrocks get to vote and make up the legislature. === Carta Accords === A statocratic republic formerly based in Carta. Only members of the military can vote, though it plans to expand to a fully civilian republic upon reclaiming the surface. Made up only of Survivors. New capital unknown. === Freedom Road === A militia group founded with the goal to free the Antrocks. They differ from the Carta Accords due to their acceptance of outsiders. They are funded and armed by M.E.G., yet remain a different group. == '''Entrances and Exits''' == === '''Entrances''' === * The only known entrance is through [[Level 11]] by traveling through a futuristic building, === '''Exits''' === * The only exit is to travel through the part of the city with 21st-century architecture, which leads back to [[Level 11]]. {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:Hurricane_LCU|Hurricane LCU]]
